payload.title: Fordham Road – Inwood Bus Priority
payload.publisher: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)
payload.description: Presentation to Community Board 5 Municipal Services Committee on proposed bus priority alternatives for Fordham Road, including offset bus lanes and busway options

tool_args.label: Bronx Community Board 5 Municipal Services Committee

payload.description: Bronx Community Board 5 Municipal Services Committee
First SBS route on Fordham Road, launched in 2008. Busiest bus route in the Bronx.
One of five routes on Fordham Road corridor
One of five routes on Fordham Road corridor
One of five routes on Fordham Road corridor

payload.limits: Major Deegan Expressway to Boston Road
payload.description: Critical crosstown transportation corridor and major Bronx-Manhattan connection. Serves 5 bus routes with 85,000 average daily riders.

Bus priority project on Fordham Road evaluating three alternatives: Alternative A (offset bus lanes), Alternative B (eastbound busway), and Alternative C (two-way busway). Advanced Alternative A as the preferred treatment.
payload.lead_agency: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
project.partner_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency is relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

tool_args.label: Alternative A: Offset Bus Lanes in Both Directions

payload.description: Convert existing curbside bus lanes to offset lanes, allowing for more curbside parking and loading zones. In effect 24/7. Single travel lane in both directions. Vehicles allowed to use offset bus lanes for right turns.

tool_args.label: Alternative B: Eastbound Busway from Morris Ave to Webster Ave

payload.description: Eastbound through traffic restrictions on Fordham Rd, except for buses and trucks, with local access on all blocks. Offset lane in westbound direction. In effect 24/7.
payload.location_text: Fordham Road from Morris Ave to Webster Ave, Bronx

normalized_location.raw_text: Fordham Road from Morris Ave to Webster Ave, Bronx

tool_args.label: Alternative C: Two-Way Busway between Jerome & Webster Aves

payload.description: Both eastbound and westbound through traffic restrictions on Fordham Rd, except for buses and trucks, with local access on all blocks. In effect 24/7.
payload.location_text: Fordham Road between Jerome Ave and Webster Ave, Bronx

normalized_location.raw_text: Fordham Road between Jerome Ave and Webster Ave, Bronx

tool_args.label: ABLE On-Bus Camera Enforcement on Bx12-SBS

payload.description: Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) implemented on Bx12-SBS buses on November 18, 2022 with a 60-day warning period. Enforces against vehicles parking or standing in a bus lane during bus lane hours.
payload.location_text: Fordham Road between Sedgwick Av and Southern Blvd

normalized_location.raw_text: Fordham Road between Sedgwick Av and Southern Blvd

tool_args.label: Existing Curbside Bus Lanes Frequently Blocked

payload.claim_text: Observations and data show that existing curbside bus lanes are frequently blocked throughout the day
payload.description: Existing curbside bus lanes on Fordham Road are frequently blocked, reducing their effectiveness

tool_args.label: ABLE Positive Effect but Insufficient Alone

payload.claim_text: ABLE bus lane enforcement is having a positive effect on bus speeds on Fordham Road. Enforcement alone has not raised bus speeds to prior levels as blockages remain frequent.
payload.description: ABLE improves bus speeds but cannot alone achieve prior speed levels

payload.claim_text: 79% support changes to city streets that can make buses faster and more reliable
payload.description: Public polling by ABNY and Change Research (Nov 2020) showed 79% of respondents support changes to make buses faster
payload.source: Association for a Better NY (ABNY) and Change Research

warnings: claim.source is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Source/program names are relation context, not claim type. Suggested relation: data_provided_by or part_of_program.

payload.claim_text: Based on factors identified in the traffic analyses and listening to the community concerns, Alternative A – offset bus lanes along Fordham Rd – is the best treatment to improve overall bus speeds, reliability, and curb demand while minimizing the effects on buses on north/south corridors
payload.description: Alternative A (offset bus lanes) was selected as the preferred treatment

payload.claim_text: Over 25 Individual Community Outreach Events to Date
payload.description: The project team conducted over 25 community outreach events including meetings, briefings, walkthroughs, and surveys
